message:
Ever wonder how your dog knows you're almost home — before your car even turns onto the street? 🐶⏰

It might be because your dog can literally smell time passing.

Here's the idea, championed by dog-cognition researcher Alexandra Horowitz: every scent in your home fades on a schedule. When you leave in the morning, your smell is strong and fresh. Hour by hour it thins out and drifts away in a steady, predictable pattern. Your dog's nose is sensitive enough to read that fading like a clock on the wall.

So as your "morning scent" reaches a certain faintness, your pup gets a nose-full of information that roughly means: they should be back about now. That's part of why so many dogs wander over to the door or window right before their human walks in.

It also helps explain the wild homecoming party you get after a long workday versus the mellow hello after a quick trip to the mailbox. To your dog, those aren't guesses — they're measurements. 🐾

So the next time your dog meets you at the door at exactly the right moment, give them a little credit: they may have been watching the clock the whole time… with their nose. 🐕💛

Does your dog always seem to know when you're coming home? Tell us below! 👇
